purpose: Documents public web sources used to corroborate narrowly scoped Phase 5 knowledge claims without rewriting preserved archive evidence.

limitations: A 2021 design update establishes the historical manufacturer count but does not enumerate all eleven names in the same passage.
supported_claims: Seven primary and four minor ship manufacturers had been defined by May 2021.
records.title: The Atlas Star Issue 3

limitations: The page is treated as a historical canonical-source snapshot rather than proof that every classification remains current.
Fimbul BYOS and Fimbul ECOS are presented as make variants and are reconciled beneath the Fimbul root family.
supported_claims: The component-variance table names eleven root manufacturer makes: Pearce, Busan, VZUS, Calico, Ogrika, Opal, Armstrong Industries, Fimbul, Tufa, Rainbow, and Floyd Line.
Fimbul BYOS and Fimbul ECOS are separately named variants.
records.title: Core Components

limitations: The page highlights selected ships and is not a complete manufacturer registry.
supported_claims: Official support identifies Pearce X4 as built by Pearce Industries and names Opal and Fimbul ships.
Floyd Liner exists as a Star Atlas ship even though it is absent from the captured 63-row dataset.
records.title: What ships can I fly in the Star Atlas UE5 Game?

limitations: The report did not state the amount of cash lost.
The approximately one-half figure describes operating runway, not necessarily one-half of total cash reserves.
supported_claims: ATMTA had material cash exposure at FTX.
Michael Wagner said ATMTA's operating runway had been reduced by approximately one-half.
records.title: Star Atlas cash runway sliced in half by FTX catastrophe

limitations: The reported loss and staffing figures are attributed to company statements and a staff memo rather than audited filings.
The source does not establish later recovery, financing, or current staffing.
supported_claims: ATMTA announced a reduction of roughly 122 positions, leaving a core team of about 45.
The report described an approximately 15 million dollar FTX loss as a significant portion of liquid cash.
The restructuring prioritized SAGE and the mobile application.
records.title: ATMTA, gaming firm behind Star Atlas, slashes 73% of workforce after FTX losses
